item.title: Examining the Hidden Variables and Costs of Transit Projects
item.description: The conversation around public transit is going through a modern renaissance. With the proliferation of social media and the unprecedented insight given into the daily lives of people around the world, there has been a surge in discussion and activity around the vitality of public transit and why its development has almost slowed down to a halt in America. The problem of building public transit - including the significant costs, red tape, and the considerable public and political will it often involves - has plagued American cities since public transit was displaced by car and highway infrastructure in national politics[1]. As the focus moved from urban centers to suburban sprawl, American political and public interest turned away from developing walkable and interconnected cities to car-centric environments that have become challenging landscapes to integrate or retrofit mass transit.

item.title: Netflix's Global Strategy
item.description: Netflix’s recent international expansion has been aggressive and marked in comparison to its competitors. As streaming platforms from fellow media giants like Disney, HBO, and ViacomCBS swarm the market - taking back their licensed content with them - Netflix is leveraging its broad subscriber base and market dominance to explore a previously untapped global audience and to create a vast library of original content in the process.

item.title: Predicting the Next Soccer Superstar
item.description: On July 27, 2011, a then 19-year-old Neymar nimbly dribbled past the Flamengo defense in the Campeonato Brasileiro and scored an extraordinary goal in the 25th minute of the game, bringing his hometown team, Santos, to a 3-0 lead. The unexpectedly brilliant goal won him the revered Puskas award that year and is widely regarded as Neymar’s arrival onto the global stage, poising him to be the next soccer superstar.
